XMVDecoder_CreateDecoderForPackets

Loads a Xbox Media Video (XMV) file from a buffer into a decoder.

HRESULT __stdcall XMVDecoder_CreateDecoderForPackets(
    DWORD Flags,
    void *pFirst4096,
    DWORD Context,
    PFNXMVGETNEXTPACKET pfnGetNextPacket,
    PFNXMVRELEASEPREVIOUSPACKET pfnReleasePreviousPacket,
    XMVDecoder **ppDecoder
);

Parameters

Flags
[in] May be zero or one of the following flags ORed together:
Value Description
XMVFLAG_UNSYNCHRONIZED_PLAYBACK Disables synchronization between the audio and the video. This flag is useful only when using XMVDecoder:GetNextFrame to play back the video. When this flag is set, regardless of the timing, the decoder will return a frame with every call to XMVDecoder::GetNextFrame (except when it's waiting for data to load).
XMVFLAG_FULL_LOOP The movie will loop until either XMVDecoder::TerminateLoop or XMVDecoder::TerminatePlayback is called.
XMVFLAG_SYNC_ON_NEXT_VBLANK Indicates that the decoder should synchronize playback on the next vertical blank. XMVDecoder::GetNextFrame will return the frame that is due before the next vertical blank, so that the frame can be prepared and displayed early. The default behavior is to return only the frame that is currently due.
pFirst4096
[in] Pointer to the first 4,096 bytes of the first packet (which contains all of the header information needed to finish setting up the decoder).
Context
[in] Context passed to the callback functions, typically a pointer to a decoder object.
pfnGetNextPacket
[in] Address of a callback function of type PFNXMVGETNEXTPACKET
.
pfnReleasePreviousPacket
[in] Address of a callback function of type PFNXMVRELEASEPREVIOUSPACKET
ppDecoder
[out] Address of a variable that receives a pointer to the XMVDecoder interface, which will decode and play the file.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful; returns an error code otherwise.

Remarks

The buffer must contain one complete packet as formatted by the XMVTool. The output .xmv file (from the tool) is formatted as follows:

// First packet
DWORD NextPacketSize;  // size of the next packet
DWORD ThisPacketSize;  // size of this packet
DWORD MaxPacketSize;   // size of the largest packet in the file
// Packet data
// ...

// All other packets
DWORD NextPacketSize;  // size of the next packet in the file
// Packet data
// ...

All sizes mentioned above include the header information inside the packets.

The loading of the first packet must be done manually because the decoder requires the first 4,096 bytes to be loaded so it can create itself. The decoder will first call the pfnGetNextPacket callback to get the first packet, and will then call pfnReleasePreviousPacket when it is safe to start loading the next packet over the previous packet.

The created decoder will call pfnGetNextPacket after it finishes decoding the final image from the current buffer to get the next buffer from which to decode. The previous buffer may still be in use and cannot be destroyed until pfnReleaseLastPacket is called. If the data is not ready, this function will return NULL.

The decoder will call pfnReleasePreviousPacket when it is completely finished with a buffer. At this point, the memory for this buffer can be safely reclaimed for other uses. Typically, the memory will be freed after pfnGetNextPacket is called to obtain the next buffer from which to decode.

During the normal process of decoding, the decoder will start with a call to pfnGetNextPacket to obtain the first packet. The decoder will then alternate calls to pfnGetNextPacket and pfnReleasePreviousPacket. The only exception is if, at the end of the video where the next packet size is zero, XMVDecoder::XMVReset is called after pfnReleasePreviousPacket is called. In this case, pfnReleasePreviousPacket will be called a second time with a nonzero NextPacketSize before pfnReleasePreviousPacket is called again.

An overlap between the previous buffer and the current buffer must occur to ensure that there is no hitch in the audio stream during playback. pfnReleasePreviousPacket will always be called after every successful call (one that does not return NULL) to pfnGetNextPacket. In fact, pfnReleasePreviousPacket will always be called after every successful call even if there is no previous buffer to be released (such as for the first frame).

This routine allocates one virtual allocation that contains all internal data structures. If the movie contains ADPCM or 5.1 audio, the memory for the packets must also be allocated as contiguous memory (using XPhysicalAlloc).

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xmv.h.

Import Library: Use Xmv.lib.

See Also

XMVTool | XMVDecoder_CreateDecoderForFile
